page: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU server instance powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 125.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.91 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 600 GB SSD, and the instance runs on the QEMU hypervisor. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the ccx53 demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core score of 3066.19 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 13.79 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic capabilities are also strong, with a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 39134.55. Memory performance is robust, scoring in the top 25% on the PassMark memory test, with uncached read and write bandwidths of 84.57 GB/sec and 62.84 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core and database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 43.41 million operations per second, perform in the average tier. LLM inference speeds are highly variable; text generation with a small 135M model reaches a top-tier 360.07 tokens per second, whereas text generation with a large 70B model drops to a poor 2.36 tokens per second due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration.

From a resource density perspective, the ccx53 offers a balanced dedicated hardware profile. By providing dedicated physical AMD EPYC Milan cores rather than shared resources, it ensures consistent execution times without noisy-neighbor interference, representing a highly stable option for production workloads. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it well-suited for general-purpose application hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations. However, the lack of a GPU and the poor performance on large-scale LLM inference make it unsuitable for heavy deep learning or large language model execution.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU server instance utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It delivers 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 125.0 GB of RAM, and 600 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on the QEMU hypervisor, the instance exhibits strong single-core and cryptographic performance, with benchmarks showing top-25% results in single-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU encryption. While multi-core processing, database operations, and memory bandwidth sit in the average tier, the dedicated resource allocation ensures predictable performance. The server's balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly efficient for production environments requiring consistent CPU performance, such as application hosting, software compilation, and web serving, though it lacks the GPU acceleration required for large-scale machine learning tasks.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated AMD EPYC Milan server featuring 32 vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and cryptographic performance for production workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated AMD EPYC Milan server with 32 vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage, optimized for consistent production workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server offering balanced compute and memory resources for predictable, production-grade application hosting.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan architecture
125.0 GB of system memory and 600 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Dedicated physical cores ensure consistent, predictable execution
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
